title: Xtra Spicy Japanese Noodles Tan Tan Udon
description: A spicy and crumbly Japanese dish featuring parsley and a mirepoix base, served in a ramekin. This vegetarian-friendly tan-tan-udon is a kid-friendly option for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Cook the udon noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery to the skillet and cook until the vegetables are tender.
Add the chopped parsley, gochujang, soy sauce, and sesame oil to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked udon noodles to the skillet and toss to combine with the vegetable mixture.
Transfer the noodle mixture to a ramekin and top with crushed red pepper.
Serve the tan-tan-udon hot and enjoy.
Garnish with additional parsley and sesame seeds if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for later use.
